subject: Spinning/Gyro operational equivalence
source_form: Source uses (back) SPIN [BOD] for BOTH 'Spinning X' (paradoxMoves) AND 'Gyro X' (gyroMoves) compounds — same operational token
modern_doctrine: Modern doctrine (per symbolicModifierEducation.ts): 'Spinning is full turn (360°). Gyro is half turn (180°)'
resolution_recommendation: surface as Red Wave 2 — gyroMoves source CONFIRMS that spinning + gyro share operational notation. The full-vs-half-turn distinction has no source operational precedent. THREE possibilities: (a) modern doctrine has drifted from source; (b) modern doctrine adds magnitude implicitly to operational tokens; (c) full-vs-half distinction was introduced post-2003
notes: This is THE Wave 2 question across 4 audits (FM FRP-5 + Job RP-6 + Paradox W2-15 + Gyro GD-1). gyroMoves provides the most direct evidence: the source corpus does NOT distinguish spinning from gyro operationally
